tax: Phylum subordinate to Elementals. Membership is assigned by mixed or para-elemental composition rather than by a single pure element.
ap: Most quasi-elementals show unstable margins: vapor venting from skin, dust-shedding joints, molten seams, or condensation halos. Their bodies often require surrounding medium to remain coherent.
eco: They reproduce or recur where elemental borders are turbulent: vent fields, ashfalls, boiling pools, collapsing caverns, and planar seams.
beh: Behavior is volatile and boundary-seeking; mephits are the chattering, social, minor order of the clade.
traitsText: Mixed elemental body, environmental dependence, resistance matching constituent element, and instability under opposed conditions.
abilities: Minor elemental exhalations, heat/cold/acid/dust effects, and short bursts of planar transition.
bg: In the Throat they mark places where the inner planes leak into geology rather than simply places where ordinary matter is hot, wet, cold, or windy.
g: Quasi-elementals are inner-planar organisms formed where elemental states mix, shear, or contaminate one another. They are not lesser true elementals but boundary creatures of steam, magma, dust, ash, salt, radiance, vacuum, and similar transitional substances.
